channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: LeadInSecurity 2026: NATO Leadership Accelerator for Women in Defence and Security (Fully Funded Mentorship Programme)
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The LeadInSecurity Programme is NATO’s Leadership Accelerator designed specifically to strengthen the next generation of women leaders in defence, security, and related strategic sectors. This initiative focuses on identifying high-potential women professionals across NATO Allied countries and equipping them with the leadership skills, mentorship, and international exposure required to thrive in complex global security environments. […]

The post LeadInSecurity 2026: NATO Leadership Accelerator for Women in Defence and Security (Fully Funded Mentorship Programme) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The LeadInSecurity Programme is NATO’s Leadership Accelerator designed specifically to strengthen the next generation of women leaders in defence, security, and related strategic sectors. This initiative focuses on identifying high-potential women professionals across NATO Allied countries and equipping them with the leadership skills, mentorship, and international exposure required to thrive in complex global security environments.

Delivered as a structured 10-month mentorship and leadership development programme (October 2026 – July 2027), LeadInSecurity provides a unique platform where participants engage directly with experienced defence and security professionals through personalised mentoring, leadership workshops, and in-person networking events.

This programme is not just a training initiative; it is a strategic investment in the future of global security leadership, ensuring that women are actively represented in decision-making roles across defence, diplomacy, international relations, and security innovation.

About LeadInSecurity by NATO

LeadInSecurity is a flagship leadership accelerator created to address the gender gap in defence and security leadership. It aims to build a strong pipeline of women leaders who can contribute meaningfully to national and international security systems.

The programme connects emerging professionals with senior experts in NATO-related defence and security fields, fostering long-term professional relationships, leadership development, and cross-border collaboration.

Key focus areas include:

  • Defence strategy and policy development
  • International security cooperation
  • Emerging security threats and global risk analysis
  • STEM applications in defence systems
  • Cybersecurity and technological security innovation
  • International relations and diplomacy

Programme Structure and Duration

The LeadInSecurity Accelerator runs for 10 months (October 2026 – July 2027) and is built around a blended learning and mentorship model.

Participants will benefit from:

  1. One-to-One Mentorship
    • Direct mentorship from experienced professionals in defence and security sectors
    • Personalized career guidance and leadership development support
  2. Workshops and Training Sessions
    • Leadership development workshops
    • Strategic thinking and decision-making training
    • Sector-specific learning modules in defence and security
  3. Networking and In-Person Events
    • Opportunities to meet NATO professionals and policymakers
    • Peer networking with emerging women leaders across Allied countries
  4. Professional Growth Exposure
    • Real-world insights into defence systems and security operations
    • Exposure to global policy discussions and strategic frameworks

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Must identify as women professionals or emerging leaders
  • Age range: 25 to 35 years old
  • Must be a national of a NATO Allied country
  • Must be working in defence, security, STEM, international relations, or closely related fields
  • Must demonstrate leadership potential and commitment to professional growth
  • Strong interest in defence policy, global security, or international cooperation
  • Ability to fully participate in a 10-month mentorship programme

Application Requirements

Applicants are required to submit:

  • Personal information (full name, age, nationality, residence)
  • Current organisation or institutional affiliation
  • Current job title or professional role
  • Email contact details
  • LinkedIn profile link

This information is used to assess eligibility and suitability for the programme’s leadership development pathway.

Key Benefits of the LeadInSecurity Programme

Selected participants will gain access to:

  • High-level NATO mentorship and leadership guidance
  • Direct engagement with defence and security professionals
  • Enhanced leadership and strategic thinking skills
  • International networking opportunities
  • Exposure to global security policy environments
  • Career development support in defence and related sectors
  • A structured 10-month professional growth journey

Programme Impact

LeadInSecurity is designed to create long-term systemic impact in global defence and security leadership by:

  • Increasing representation of women in strategic security roles
  • Strengthening leadership pipelines in NATO Allied countries
  • Promoting diversity in defence and security decision-making
  • Supporting cross-border collaboration and knowledge exchange

Deadline

The application deadline for LeadInSecurity 2026 is:
5 July 2026

Apply Now

To learn more and apply, Click Here

Conclusion

The LeadInSecurity Programme represents a rare opportunity for women professionals in defence, security, and international relations to gain direct access to NATO-level leadership development. With its structured mentorship model, global exposure, and high-level networking opportunities, it serves as a powerful accelerator for future leaders shaping international security policy.

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post LeadInSecurity 2026: NATO Leadership Accelerator for Women in Defence and Security (Fully Funded Mentorship Programme) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: TiE Women Program 2026: Global Competition Empowering Women Entrepreneurs with Funding, Mentorship, and Investor Access
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The TiE Women Program 2026 is a prestigious global entrepreneurship initiative designed exclusively for women founders and women-led startups who are ready to scale their businesses, strengthen their leadership journey, and gain international visibility. This program serves as one of the most influential platforms for women entrepreneurs across the world to connect with investors, experienced […]

The post TiE Women Program 2026: Global Competition Empowering Women Entrepreneurs with Funding, Mentorship, and Investor Access appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The TiE Women Program 2026 is a prestigious global entrepreneurship initiative designed exclusively for women founders and women-led startups who are ready to scale their businesses, strengthen their leadership journey, and gain international visibility. This program serves as one of the most influential platforms for women entrepreneurs across the world to connect with investors, experienced mentors, and a global entrepreneurial ecosystem.

Organized by TiE Women (tiewomen.org) under the broader TiE Global network, the program is built to identify, support, and elevate women-led startups through structured mentoring, regional competitions, and a global pitch finale where outstanding founders compete for equity-free funding and global recognition.

Overview of TiE Women Program 2026

The TiE Women Program is more than just a competition—it is a global entrepreneurial growth journey. It is designed to support women founders at the early and scaling stages of business development by offering:

  • Access to global mentorship from successful entrepreneurs
  • Exposure to international investors and funding opportunities
  • A structured program of learning, pitching, and scaling
  • A platform to showcase innovative startup solutions
  • Entry into a global community of women entrepreneurs

The program officially runs from 8 March 2026 to 25 June 2026, with regional and global stages continuing through December 2026.

Purpose and Vision of the Program

The core mission of TiE Women is to empower women entrepreneurs across the world regardless of industry, background, or geography. The initiative is built on key pillars:

  • Learning and entrepreneurial education
  • Mentorship from experienced business leaders
  • Access to funding opportunities
  • Business scalability and growth support
  • Creation of a safe, inclusive entrepreneurial ecosystem
  • Global networking and community building

The program aims to close the gender gap in entrepreneurship by giving women founders equal access to capital, mentorship, and global platforms.

Program Benefits for Participants

Participants selected for the TiE Women Program 2026 gain access to a wide range of high-value entrepreneurial opportunities:

1. Financial Opportunity

  • Equity-free cash prize of up to $50,000 for winners
  • Exposure to global investors and venture capital networks

2. Mentorship and Learning

  • One-on-one and group mentoring by experienced entrepreneurs
  • Strategic guidance on scaling startups
  • Sessions on fundraising, growth strategy, and leadership

3. Global Exposure

  • Opportunity to present on a global stage
  • Participation in regional and international pitch competitions
  • Visibility through TiE’s global network

4. Networking and Community

  • Access to thousands of entrepreneurs and industry leaders
  • Collaboration opportunities across global TiE chapters
  • Connection to startup ecosystems in over 60 countries

5. Business Growth Support

  • Structured entrepreneurial development journey
  • Investor readiness preparation
  • Feedback from industry experts and judges

Program Timeline and Key Stages

The TiE Women Program 2026 follows a structured global competition pathway:

  1. Applications Open: 8 March 2026
  2. Applications Close: 25 June 2026
  3. Chapter Mentoring & Regional Finals: 16 June – 15 August 2026
  4. Global Knowledge Series: 16 August – 15 September 2026
  5. Semi-Finals (USA & Asia): 24 September – 7 October 2026
  6. Global Grand Finale: December 2026 (TiE Global Summit)

The final stage brings together top women entrepreneurs from around the world to pitch their startups for the grand prize.

Eligibility Criteria

To ensure fairness and alignment with the program’s mission, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Only female-founded or female co-founded startups are eligible
  • Female co-founder(s) must hold at least 33% equity in the company
  • The business must be less than 7 years old (registered after 1 January 2019)
  • Startups must preferably be in fundraising or growth stage
  • Idea-stage startups are not eligible

The program welcomes applications from all industries and sectors.

Why TiE Women Program Matters

The TiE Women initiative has already created measurable global impact:

  • Over 11,000 applications from 62 countries
  • More than 500 women-led startups accelerated
  • Over 1,000 entrepreneurs mentored
  • Over $350,000+ in equity-free funding distributed
  • Active global network across 57+ locations worldwide

This demonstrates its strong influence in shaping the future of women-led entrepreneurship globally.

Apply Now

Applications open on 8 March 2026 and close on 25 June 2026. Early application is strongly recommended due to competitive selection across thousands of global applicants.

Apply Here

Important Disclaimer

TiE Women Program does not charge any application or participation fee. Any request for payment related to application support or pitch deck creation is not authorized. Selection is strictly based on merit, and all final decisions are made by the program organizers.

For more information, visit the official website

Dsicover more global competition opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post TiE Women Program 2026: Global Competition Empowering Women Entrepreneurs with Funding, Mentorship, and Investor Access appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Join the 180 Degrees Consulting Global Leadership Team 2026: Lead Global Social Impact While Developing World-Class Leadership Skills
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
180 Degrees Consulting is inviting passionate, purpose-driven individuals from around the world to apply for positions within its Global Leadership Team (GLT). This unique opportunity allows emerging and experienced leaders to contribute to one of the world’s largest student-driven consulting organizations while helping social enterprises, nonprofits, and socially conscious organizations create meaningful impact. If you […]

The post Join the 180 Degrees Consulting Global Leadership Team 2026: Lead Global Social Impact While Developing World-Class Leadership Skills appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
180 Degrees Consulting is inviting passionate, purpose-driven individuals from around the world to apply for positions within its Global Leadership Team (GLT). This unique opportunity allows emerging and experienced leaders to contribute to one of the world’s largest student-driven consulting organizations while helping social enterprises, nonprofits, and socially conscious organizations create meaningful impact.

If you are passionate about social impact, leadership, international collaboration, consulting, organizational growth, and personal development, the 180 Degrees Consulting Global Leadership Team offers a platform to make a difference on a global scale.

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and successful candidates will join a dynamic, diverse, and fully remote international leadership community dedicated to creating positive change worldwide.

What is 180 Degrees Consulting?

180 Degrees Consulting is a global organization that supports nonprofits and socially conscious organizations through high-quality consulting services. The organization empowers students and professionals to solve critical social challenges while building valuable leadership and consulting experience.

With branches and members operating across numerous countries, 180 Degrees Consulting has established itself as a leading platform for social impact consulting, helping organizations maximize their effectiveness and achieve sustainable outcomes.

At the heart of this mission is the Global Leadership Team (GLT), the body responsible for guiding and managing the organization’s worldwide operations and strategic direction.

What is the Global Leadership Team (GLT)?

The Global Leadership Team serves as the central leadership body of 180 Degrees Consulting worldwide.

Members of the GLT contribute to a wide range of strategic and operational initiatives, including:

  • Supporting existing 180 Degrees Consulting branches globally.
  • Assisting in the establishment of new branches.
  • Facilitating collaboration across international branches.
  • Maintaining and improving consulting quality standards.
  • Measuring and enhancing global social impact.
  • Developing systems and processes that enable organizational growth.
  • Driving innovation and organizational effectiveness.
  • Making strategic decisions that shape the future of the organization.

This role provides a unique opportunity to influence a global organization while collaborating with talented individuals from diverse cultural and professional backgrounds.

Why Join the 180 Degrees Consulting Global Leadership Team?

1. Create Meaningful Social Impact

One of the most compelling reasons to join the GLT is the opportunity to contribute directly to positive social change.

As a team member, your efforts will help:

  • Strengthen nonprofit organizations.
  • Support social enterprises.
  • Empower students worldwide.
  • Expand access to consulting services for mission-driven organizations.
  • Increase the global social impact of 180 Degrees Consulting.

Every project, initiative, and decision contributes toward improving communities and organizations around the world.

2. Accelerate Your Personal Development

GLT members gain hands-on experience in areas that are highly valued by employers and graduate programs.

Key skills developed include:

  • Leadership
  • Strategic thinking
  • Project management
  • Consulting
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Persuasive communication
  • Negotiation
  • Data analysis
  • Team management
  • Organizational development

These experiences can significantly strengthen your professional profile and future career opportunities.

3. Collaborate with a Diverse Global Community

The Global Leadership Team includes members from countries across the world, creating a truly international working environment.

Benefits of this global exposure include:

  • Cross-cultural collaboration.
  • International networking opportunities.
  • Exposure to diverse perspectives.
  • Development of global leadership capabilities.
  • Building lifelong professional relationships.

Team members regularly work with colleagues from regions spanning Latin America, Europe, Africa, Asia, Oceania, and North America.

4. Enjoy Flexible Remote Work

The GLT operates as a remote-first organization.

This allows members to:

  • Work from anywhere in the world.
  • Balance commitments with studies or full-time employment.
  • Participate in meaningful work without relocation.
  • Manage schedules across multiple time zones.

The organization values flexibility while maintaining a strong commitment to impact and accountability.

5. Enhance Your Career Prospects

The experience gained through the Global Leadership Team can help members pursue careers in:

  • Consulting
  • Business development
  • Social impact
  • Nonprofit leadership
  • Data analytics
  • Strategy
  • International development
  • Project management
  • Marketing
  • Public policy

Employers increasingly value candidates who demonstrate leadership experience within international organizations.

Core Values of the Global Leadership Team

The culture of 180 Degrees Consulting is built on several guiding principles:

We Are Eager to Learn

Members actively seek opportunities for continuous learning and improvement.

We Take Ownership

Team members demonstrate initiative, responsibility, and self-leadership.

We Are Inclusive

The organization embraces diversity and fosters respect for people from all backgrounds.

We Are Impact Driven

Every activity is guided by a commitment to maximizing positive social impact.

We Take Care of Ourselves and Each Other

The organization promotes well-being, collaboration, and mutual support.

The Global Leadership Team Recruitment Process

180 Degrees Consulting follows a structured recruitment process designed to identify candidates who align with its mission and values.

Step 1: Online Application

Applicants submit information about:

  • Professional experience
  • Academic background
  • Leadership experience
  • Motivation for joining
  • Potential contributions to the GLT

The organization encourages authenticity and purpose-driven applications.

Step 2: Personal Interview

Shortlisted candidates meet with a member of the People & Culture Team.

The interview explores:

  • Personal motivations
  • Communication style
  • Collaboration skills
  • Leadership mindset
  • Alignment with organizational values

Applicants can also ask questions about the organization and available roles.

Step 3: Technical Assessment and Interview

Candidates may complete a practical task related to their chosen position.

The assessment evaluates:

  • Problem-solving abilities
  • Strategic thinking
  • Communication skills
  • Decision-making capabilities
  • Technical competencies

Candidates then discuss their work with relevant team leaders.

The organization remains committed to accessibility and fairness throughout the recruitment process.

Step 4: Cultural Interview

The final stage focuses on cultural fit.

Applicants meet with executive leadership to discuss:

  • Working style
  • Team collaboration
  • Organizational values
  • Commitment to social impact
  • Ability to thrive in a remote environment

Important Application Considerations

Time Commitment

Successful applicants should be prepared to commit:

  • Approximately 7–10 hours per week.
  • A minimum of 12 months of service.

Consistency and accountability are highly valued, although the organization aims to remain flexible and supportive.

Who Can Apply?

180 Degrees Consulting welcomes applicants from:

  • All academic disciplines.
  • Diverse professional backgrounds.
  • Different cultures and nationalities.
  • Various levels of consulting experience.

Formal consulting experience is not required.

The organization prioritizes:

  • Passion for social impact.
  • Curiosity and willingness to learn.
  • Collaboration skills.
  • Commitment to contributing meaningfully.

Remote Global Collaboration

The GLT operates across more than 60 countries using tools such as:

  • Slack
  • Notion
  • Zoom

Successful members typically demonstrate:

  • Strong self-motivation.
  • Effective virtual communication.
  • Time management skills.
  • Ownership and accountability.

Recruitment Timeline

The recruitment process generally takes:

  • Approximately 3–4 weeks from application to final decision.

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis.

Early applications are encouraged.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should carefully review available position descriptions before submitting an application.

Applicants should ensure they understand:

  • Role requirements
  • Eligibility criteria
  • Time commitments
  • Required competencies

Applications can be submitted through the official 180 Degrees Consulting website.

Apply Here

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

The 180 Degrees Consulting Global Leadership Team offers far more than a typical volunteer role. It provides a platform to:

  • Lead international initiatives.
  • Influence organizational strategy.
  • Build leadership and consulting expertise.
  • Work alongside talented professionals worldwide.
  • Contribute to measurable social impact.
  • Expand professional networks.
  • Develop skills valued by employers and graduate institutions.

For individuals seeking a meaningful leadership experience that combines professional growth with social impact, the Global Leadership Team represents an exceptional opportunity.

For more information, visit the official website. 

Discover more global volunteer opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Join the 180 Degrees Consulting Global Leadership Team 2026: Lead Global Social Impact While Developing World-Class Leadership Skills appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: British Council Next Generation Pulse Bangladesh 2026: Apply for a Research Consultancy Opportunity to Study Youth Attitudes, Employment, Education, and Civic Engagement
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The British Council has announced an exciting research consultancy opportunity under its newly launched Next Generation Pulse initiative. Organizations with expertise in youth research, policy analysis, education, employment, and social development are invited to submit proposals to conduct a comprehensive study focused on young people in Bangladesh. This consultancy opportunity offers researchers and institutions the […]

The post British Council Next Generation Pulse Bangladesh 2026: Apply for a Research Consultancy Opportunity to Study Youth Attitudes, Employment, Education, and Civic Engagement appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The British Council has announced an exciting research consultancy opportunity under its newly launched Next Generation Pulse initiative. Organizations with expertise in youth research, policy analysis, education, employment, and social development are invited to submit proposals to conduct a comprehensive study focused on young people in Bangladesh.

This consultancy opportunity offers researchers and institutions the chance to contribute to one of the British Council’s most important youth-focused research initiatives, generating evidence that can help shape future policy decisions, educational reforms, and youth development strategies.

The study will serve as the pilot edition of the Next Generation Pulse programme and will build upon previous Next Generation research conducted in Bangladesh. Through this initiative, the British Council seeks timely, actionable insights into the lives, aspirations, challenges, and experiences of young people in Bangladesh.

About the British Council Next Generation Pulse Initiative

The British Council’s Next Generation research programme has become a respected source of evidence on youth perspectives across multiple countries. Building on the success of the broader Next Generation series, the organization is introducing a new and more agile research model called Next Generation Pulse.

The purpose of Pulse is to provide:

  • Faster research cycles
  • Timely insights into youth experiences
  • Cost-effective evidence gathering
  • Action-oriented findings
  • Policy-relevant recommendations
  • Comparative analysis with previous studies

Bangladesh has been selected as the first pilot country for this innovative research model.

The findings generated through the study will not only provide valuable insights into current youth realities but will also contribute to shaping the future direction of the wider Next Generation research portfolio.

Why Bangladesh?

Bangladesh is currently experiencing significant demographic, economic, educational, and social transitions. Young people constitute a large share of the country’s population, making youth-focused research critically important.

The British Council is particularly interested in understanding how young people are navigating:

  • Educational opportunities
  • Employment challenges
  • Civic participation
  • Political engagement
  • Skills development
  • Social and cultural transformations

The study aims to provide evidence-based insights that can help policymakers, educators, employers, development organizations, and youth advocates better understand and respond to the needs of Bangladesh’s youth population.

Key Research Themes

The selected research supplier will be expected to investigate several critical themes affecting young people in Bangladesh.

1. Youth Lifestyles and Attitudes

The research should explore:

  • Youth aspirations
  • Social values
  • Future expectations
  • Personal priorities
  • Lifestyle trends
  • Social influences
  • Perceptions of opportunities and challenges

Understanding youth attitudes provides important context for future policy and programme development.

2. Youth Voice and Participation

The study should examine:

  • Political engagement
  • Civic participation
  • Community involvement
  • Democratic participation
  • Trust in institutions
  • Youth leadership
  • Social activism

These findings will help stakeholders understand how young people engage with public life and influence societal change.

3. Education, Skills, and Employment

One of the most important focus areas is the relationship between education and employment.

The research should explore:

  • Access to education
  • Skills development
  • Employability challenges
  • Workforce readiness
  • Career aspirations
  • Skills gaps
  • Youth labor market experiences

4. Youth Unemployment Crisis

The British Council has identified youth unemployment as a key area of interest.

The study should investigate:

  • Barriers to employment
  • Labor market realities
  • Job search experiences
  • Underemployment
  • Economic participation
  • Career opportunities
  • Skills mismatch

5. Education Quality and Relevance

The consultancy should also examine:

  • Educational quality
  • Curriculum relevance
  • Skills alignment with labor market demands
  • Student preparedness
  • Transition from education to employment
  • Perceived value of educational qualifications

Expected Research Outputs

The selected supplier will be responsible for producing several deliverables that meet British Council standards.

Research Dataset

The consultant must provide:

  • Aggregated datasets
  • Demographic analysis
  • Structured data outputs
  • Data segmentation agreed upon with the British Council

Topline Findings and Analysis

The study should produce:

  • Initial findings
  • Key trends
  • Analytical insights
  • Policy-relevant observations

Comprehensive Final Report

The final report must:

  • Be written in English
  • Be delivered in Word and PDF formats
  • Be professionally edited and proofread
  • Follow British Council branding guidelines

Executive Summary

A concise summary should include:

  • Key findings
  • Major conclusions
  • Policy implications
  • Strategic recommendations

PowerPoint Presentation

Researchers must develop:

  • A presentation deck
  • Visual summaries
  • Key data points
  • Strategic insights

Infographics

The consultancy includes the creation of:

  • 4–5 visual infographics
  • Social-media-friendly graphics
  • Easy-to-understand data visualizations
  • Public dissemination materials

Important Timeline and Deadlines

Organizations interested in applying should pay close attention to the official procurement schedule.

Key Dates

Request for Proposals Issued

  • 22 May 2026

Deadline for Clarification Questions

  • 1 June 2026 (12:00 UK Time)

British Council Responses to Questions

  • 3 June 2026

Proposal Submission Deadline

  • 19 June 2026 (12:00 UK Time)

Optional Presentations

  • 30 June 2026

Best and Final Offer Submission

  • 1 July 2026

Final Decision Announcement

  • 6 July 2026

Contract Award

  • 13 July 2026

Contract Start Date

  • 14 July 2026

Why This Opportunity Matters

This consultancy represents more than a traditional research contract.

Successful applicants will have the opportunity to:

  1. Influence youth policy discussions in Bangladesh.
  2. Contribute to an internationally recognized research programme.
  3. Generate evidence that informs education and employment strategies.
  4. Support understanding of youth aspirations and challenges.
  5. Help shape future British Council research initiatives.
  6. Gain visibility through a high-profile international project.

How to Apply

Interested suppliers must submit clarification requests and final proposals through the British Council procurement platform.

Applicants should ensure all documents are submitted before the proposal deadline.

The British Council recommends that suppliers familiarize themselves with procurement requirements and technical submission procedures well in advance.

Apply Here

Final Thoughts

The British Council Next Generation Pulse Bangladesh consultancy presents an important opportunity for research organizations and institutions interested in youth development, education, employment, and public policy.

As Bangladesh becomes the first pilot country for the Next Generation Pulse model, the selected research partner will play a critical role in generating evidence that can influence decision-making and contribute to a deeper understanding of the realities facing young people today.

Organizations with strong expertise in research design, data collection, analysis, and youth-focused studies are encouraged to prepare competitive proposals before the submission deadline of 19 June 2026.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post British Council Next Generation Pulse Bangladesh 2026: Apply for a Research Consultancy Opportunity to Study Youth Attitudes, Employment, Education, and Civic Engagement appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Queen’s University Belfast FORESIGHTERS Fully Funded PhD Programme 2026 in the UK | 20 Doctoral Positions with Salary Up to £37,734
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
Applications are now officially open for the highly prestigious FORESIGHTERS Doctoral Training Programme 2026 at Queen’s University Belfast in the United Kingdom. The programme is offering 20 fully funded PhD positions for early-stage researchers interested in interdisciplinary, future-focused research designed to tackle some of the world’s most pressing societal challenges. Funded by Horizon Europe MSCA […]

The post Queen’s University Belfast FORESIGHTERS Fully Funded PhD Programme 2026 in the UK | 20 Doctoral Positions with Salary Up to £37,734 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now officially open for the highly prestigious FORESIGHTERS Doctoral Training Programme 2026 at Queen’s University Belfast in the United Kingdom. The programme is offering 20 fully funded PhD positions for early-stage researchers interested in interdisciplinary, future-focused research designed to tackle some of the world’s most pressing societal challenges.

Funded by Horizon Europe MSCA COFUND and the Department for the Economy (Northern Ireland), FORESIGHTERS provides a unique doctoral training experience that combines world-class academic research, interdisciplinary collaboration, Futures Thinking, and engagement with non-academic partners across multiple sectors.

Successful candidates will receive full tuition coverage, a highly competitive salary package, staff employment benefits, research and travel funding, international collaboration opportunities, and advanced training designed to prepare the next generation of global research leaders.

The application deadline is Monday, 29th June 2026.

What is the FORESIGHTERS Programme?

FORESIGHTERS stands for:

Future ORiEnted Skills, Innovation for Governance for Health, Technology, EneRgy, citieS and creativity

The programme has been developed to train researchers capable of understanding and responding to complex global changes through interdisciplinary and future-oriented research.

Unlike traditional PhD programmes, FORESIGHTERS is centered around:

  • Futures Thinking
  • Futures Literacy
  • Interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Societal impact
  • Innovation and governance
  • Sustainability and resilience

Researchers will learn how to critically explore multiple possible futures and apply future-focused methodologies to today’s societal, technological, environmental, and governance challenges.

Number of Fully Funded Positions

Queen’s University Belfast is recruiting:

  • 20 Early-Stage Researchers (ESRs)
  • 42-month full-time funded doctoral positions

There are:

  • 5 research themes
  • 4 vacancies available within each research theme

Applicants apply directly to one of the research themes and compete within that applicant pool.

Research Themes Available

Applicants can apply under one of the following interdisciplinary research themes:

1. Health Research Theme

Focused on healthcare innovation, governance, wellbeing, public health, health systems, and future healthcare solutions.

2. Technology Research Theme

Exploring artificial intelligence, emerging technologies, digital transformation, governance, ethics, and technological futures.

3. Energy Research Theme

Research involving sustainability, climate transitions, renewable energy systems, resilience, and future energy solutions.

4. Future Cities Research Theme

Projects addressing smart cities, urban planning, infrastructure, sustainability, resilience, and future urban development.

5. Creativity Research Theme

Connecting arts, culture, humanities, innovation, storytelling, and creative futures.

Salary and Financial Benefits

The FORESIGHTERS programme offers an exceptional financial package for doctoral researchers.

Successful applicants will receive:

Competitive Salary Package

  • Salary package up to £37,734
  • Additional family allowance of £6,600 for eligible researchers

Full Tuition Fee Coverage

  • Complete PhD tuition funding
  • Access to advanced doctoral training resources

Research and Development Funding

Researchers will also receive:

  • Dedicated research funding
  • Travel funding
  • Opportunities for secondments with external organizations
  • International networking opportunities

Employment Benefits

Researchers are appointed on full-time employment contracts and receive:

  • Staff employment benefits
  • Access to university staff development programmes
  • Professional skills training
  • Career development support

Why the FORESIGHTERS Programme is Unique

FORESIGHTERS goes beyond conventional doctoral research.

The programme combines:

  • Academic excellence
  • Futures Thinking methodologies
  • Interdisciplinary supervision
  • Real-world collaboration
  • Policy and societal engagement

Researchers will work with:

  • Academic supervisors
  • Industry partners
  • Public sector organizations
  • Non-academic collaborators

The programme encourages co-designed research projects that create real-world impact beyond academia.

Open Research Call Structure

One of the most unique aspects of the programme is that it operates as an open call.

Applicants may:

  • Select from suggested project ideas
  • Develop and propose their own research ideas

This gives researchers significant intellectual independence while ensuring their ideas align with the broader aims of the FORESIGHTERS programme.

Applicants are encouraged to:

  • Highlight interdisciplinary links
  • Demonstrate originality
  • Show societal relevance
  • Connect their proposal to future-oriented thinking

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for MSCA funding, applicants must answer “No” to BOTH of the following questions:

Mobility Rule

Applicants must NOT have:

  • Resided in the UK
  • Carried out their main education
  • Or undertaken employment activity in the UK

for more than 12 months within the 36 months before the application deadline (29th June 2026).

Doctoral Degree Rule

Applicants must NOT:

  • Already possess a doctoral degree
  • Be in the process of being awarded one before the application deadline

Applicants who do not meet these requirements will be considered ineligible.

Equality, Diversity, and Inclusion Commitment

Queen’s University Belfast strongly emphasizes equality of opportunity and inclusive recruitment practices.

The university is internationally recognized for:

  • Gender equality leadership
  • Diversity and inclusion initiatives
  • Disability inclusion
  • Equitable employment practices

FORESIGHTERS specifically commits to:

  • Diverse selection panels
  • Inclusive recruitment strategies
  • Accessible interview processes
  • Recognition of cultural and language differences
  • Fair assessment of applicants with career breaks, parental leave, or non-traditional pathways

The programme explicitly encourages applications from underrepresented groups in academia.

Detailed Recruitment Timeline

Recruitment Opens

18th May 2026

Information Webinars

10th June 2026

Application Deadline

29th June 2026

Eligibility Screening

Expected within 2 weeks after applications close

Shortlisting Notifications

Expected by end of July 2026

Online Research Theme Sandpits

Expected in early September 2026

Interviews

Expected in early October 2026

Final Appointment Decisions

Expected by November 2026

Programme Start Date

January 2027

Step-by-Step Application Process

Stage 1: Online Application Submission

Applications must be submitted through the Research section of the Queen’s University Belfast Career Opportunities portal.

Applicants must complete:

  • Personal information
  • Contact details
  • Employment history
  • References
  • Equal opportunities section
  • Declaration forms

Required Application Documents

Applicants must upload a single combined file containing:

1. Curriculum Vitae (Maximum 2 Pages)

The CV should include:

  • Academic qualifications
  • Universities attended
  • Degrees awarded
  • Grades and courses
  • Professional qualifications
  • Employment experience
  • Research and voluntary experience
  • Publications (if any)

2. Supporting Statement (Maximum 2 Pages)

Applicants should explain:

  • How they meet the essential and desirable criteria
  • Motivation for joining FORESIGHTERS
  • Interest in interdisciplinary doctoral research

3. Outline Research Proposal (Maximum 1 Page)

The proposal should:

  • Describe research interests
  • Align with the chosen research theme
  • Highlight interdisciplinary links where relevant
  • Demonstrate originality and future-focused thinking

Applicants can apply to multiple themes, but a separate application must be submitted for each theme.

Stage 2: Eligibility Screening and Shortlisting

Applications are reviewed for:

  • MSCA eligibility compliance
  • Academic suitability
  • Research potential
  • Quality of supporting documents
  • Strength of research proposal

Each application is reviewed by at least two panel members using a structured scoring rubric.

Approximately:

  • 6–8 applicants per theme
    will be shortlisted for interview.

Stage 3: Online Sandpits and Interview Assessment

Shortlisted applicants will participate in:

  • Online Research Theme Information Sandpits

These sessions are designed to:

  • Introduce potential supervisors
  • Explore interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Encourage co-design with non-academic partners
  • Help applicants refine their research ideas

Importantly:
Candidates are NOT assessed during the Sandpit sessions.

Final Research Proposal

Before interviews, shortlisted applicants must submit a more detailed research proposal demonstrating:

  • Originality
  • Research impact
  • Interdisciplinary thinking
  • Methodology and feasibility
  • Evidence of refinement after Sandpit participation

Interview Structure

All interviews last approximately one hour.

Interview Components

Welcome and Introductions

14 minutes

Research Proposal Presentation

  • 3 slides
  • 3-minute presentation

Futures Thinking Task

Candidates receive a special interdisciplinary task 60 minutes before the interview and present:

  • 3-minute presentation
  • No PowerPoint allowed

Panel Questions

40 minutes

Assessment criteria include:

  • Excellence
  • Interdisciplinarity
  • Impact
  • Research potential
  • Communication skills

Stage 4: Final Appointment

Following interviews:

  • The top 4 candidates in each research theme will receive offers
  • Additional appointable candidates may be placed on reserve lists

Successful applicants will:

  • Finalize their research projects
  • Confirm supervisory teams
  • Receive support for UK visa applications if required

The university will assist international researchers with:

  • Global Talent Visa support
  • Relocation processes
  • UK work authorization requirements

Why Study at Queen’s University Belfast?

Queen’s University Belfast is one of the UK’s leading research-intensive universities and a member of the prestigious Russell Group.

The university is recognized globally for:

  • Academic excellence
  • Research impact
  • Sustainability leadership
  • Diversity and inclusion
  • Doctoral training quality

Global Recognition

Queen’s University Belfast is:

  • Ranked among the world’s top 200 universities
  • Recognized in the QS Sustainability Rankings 2026
  • Holder of the Athena Swan Gold Award
  • Recognized as a University of Sanctuary

Living in Belfast

Belfast offers:

  • Affordable living costs compared to many UK cities
  • Strong international student community
  • Vibrant arts and culture
  • Excellent quality of life
  • Rich history and modern innovation ecosystem

Final Thoughts

The FORESIGHTERS Doctoral Training Programme 2026 represents one of the most exciting fully funded interdisciplinary PhD opportunities currently available in Europe.

With generous funding, strong institutional support, interdisciplinary collaboration, Futures Thinking training, and global research engagement, the programme is designed to prepare researchers to become leaders capable of shaping a more sustainable, innovative, and resilient future.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply through the official application portal HERE.

Click HERE for more information about the Belfast FORESIGHTERS Fully Funded PhD Programme.

Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here.

The post Queen’s University Belfast FORESIGHTERS Fully Funded PhD Programme 2026 in the UK | 20 Doctoral Positions with Salary Up to £37,734 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Internet Law and Policy Foundry Fellowship 2026–2028: Fully Detailed Guide to the Prestigious Global Professional Development Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The Internet Law and Policy Foundry (ILPF) has officially opened applications for its highly competitive 2026–2028 Class 8 Fellowship Program. Designed for emerging leaders working at the intersection of law, technology, policy, governance, and digital innovation, this two-year professional development fellowship provides an exceptional platform for participants to expand their expertise, grow their networks, and […]

The post Internet Law and Policy Foundry Fellowship 2026–2028: Fully Detailed Guide to the Prestigious Global Professional Development Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Internet Law and Policy Foundry (ILPF) has officially opened applications for its highly competitive 2026–2028 Class 8 Fellowship Program. Designed for emerging leaders working at the intersection of law, technology, policy, governance, and digital innovation, this two-year professional development fellowship provides an exceptional platform for participants to expand their expertise, grow their networks, and contribute meaningfully to conversations shaping the future of the internet.

As governments, companies, civil society organizations, and international institutions continue to grapple with rapidly evolving technological challenges, there is an increasing need for informed professionals who can bridge the gap between technology and public policy. The Internet Law and Policy Foundry Fellowship aims to cultivate exactly this type of leadership.

The fellowship welcomes applications from students, researchers, legal professionals, policy practitioners, academics, advocates, and technology professionals from around the world who are passionate about internet governance, digital rights, artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, privacy, online safety, telecommunications, and related fields.

About the Internet Law and Policy Foundry

The Internet Law and Policy Foundry is a professional development organization dedicated to fostering the next generation of leaders in internet law and technology policy. Through mentorship, networking opportunities, collaborative projects, events, publications, and committee participation, the Foundry creates an environment where diverse perspectives can engage in constructive dialogue and contribute to solving pressing digital policy challenges.

One of the organization’s core objectives is to identify talented individuals from various professional, academic, and geographic backgrounds and help them develop into influential voices within the broader technology policy ecosystem.

The Foundry actively encourages participation from individuals with diverse experiences, viewpoints, and professional trajectories, recognizing that effective internet governance requires inclusive and multidisciplinary collaboration.

Fellowship Structure

The Foundry Fellowship is a two-year unpaid professional development program.

While the fellowship is largely conducted remotely, participants typically gather in person with other fellows in their region whenever opportunities arise. Fellows may also participate in larger technology policy events and conferences, including opportunities to engage with the wider Foundry community at major industry gatherings such as the State of the Net Conference.

Junior Fellows are expected to contribute approximately 3–5 hours per week toward fellowship activities, programming, and committee responsibilities.

Although there is no residency requirement, applicants should be aware that most Foundry activities occur during North American business hours, particularly Eastern Time (ET). Successful applicants should therefore be prepared to contribute during those time periods.

Committee Opportunities

All applicants are required to rank their preferred committee assignments. These committees play a critical role in advancing the Foundry’s mission and provide fellows with valuable leadership and professional development experiences.

Tech Policy Grind Podcast Committee

This committee manages the Foundry’s podcast production and content development. Members help identify discussion topics, coordinate guest speakers, develop episode concepts, and collaborate on outreach efforts. Participants gain hands-on experience in media production, thought leadership, stakeholder engagement, and policy communications.

Events & Fundraising Committee

The Events & Fundraising Committee is responsible for planning and executing many of the Foundry’s flagship programs and networking opportunities. Members help organize conferences, professional events, hackathons, social gatherings, sponsorship activities, and other initiatives that bring together the technology policy community.

Editorial & Content Committee

This committee develops original written content for publication. Fellows contribute articles, policy analyses, legal commentary, opinion pieces, case summaries, technical handbooks, policy guides, and other thought leadership materials. This committee offers an excellent opportunity for individuals seeking to strengthen their research, writing, and policy communication skills.

Communications & Marketing Committee

Members of this committee support the Foundry’s outreach efforts, newsletter development, branding initiatives, social media engagement, website management, and professional communications. Fellows gain practical experience in strategic communications while helping amplify the work of the Foundry community.

Why Apply?

The Internet Law and Policy Foundry Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to become part of a vibrant network of professionals shaping the future of technology governance.

Benefits include:

  • Access to a global network of technology policy professionals
  • Professional development and leadership opportunities
  • Collaboration with experts across law, government, academia, civil society, and industry
  • Opportunities to publish original work
  • Participation in technology policy events and conferences
  • Development of practical skills in communications, policy analysis, event management, research, and advocacy
  • Exposure to emerging issues in internet governance and technology regulation
  • Long-term career development support

Areas of Interest

The fellowship is particularly relevant for individuals interested in:

  • Artificial Intelligence Governance
  • Internet Governance
  • Cybersecurity Policy
  • Data Privacy and Protection
  • Platform Accountability
  • Digital Rights
  • Telecommunications Policy
  • Content Moderation
  • Online Safety
  • Technology Regulation
  • Digital Trade
  • Emerging Technology Law

Application Timeline

Applications Open: June 1, 2026

Application Deadline: June 22, 2026, at 11:59 PM Eastern Time (ET)

Selection Results: Mid-July 2026

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, meaning early submissions are strongly encouraged.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should complete the online application form, submit their resume or CV in PDF format, rank their committee preferences, and respond to several written essay questions focused on technology policy, leadership, diversity, and professional interests.

Applicants will also be asked to discuss how their experiences and perspectives can contribute to the Foundry community and advance diversity and inclusion within the internet law and policy field.

For professionals and students seeking meaningful engagement in technology governance, policy development, and digital innovation, the Internet Law and Policy Foundry Fellowship represents a valuable opportunity to build expertise, expand professional networks, and contribute to shaping the future of the digital world.

Deadline: June 22, 2026

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here

For more global opportunities, click here.

 

The post Internet Law and Policy Foundry Fellowship 2026–2028: Fully Detailed Guide to the Prestigious Global Professional Development Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Mary Douglas Research Scholarships 2026 at UCL: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Anthropology in the United Kingdom
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The Mary Douglas Research Scholarships 2026 at University College London (UCL) present an exceptional opportunity for aspiring researchers seeking fully funded doctoral study in anthropology. Offered through UCL Anthropology and supported by the generous funding of the Sigrid Rausing Trust, these prestigious scholarships are designed to support outstanding scholars pursuing innovative and impactful research at […]

The post Mary Douglas Research Scholarships 2026 at UCL: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Anthropology in the United Kingdom appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Mary Douglas Research Scholarships 2026 at University College London (UCL) present an exceptional opportunity for aspiring researchers seeking fully funded doctoral study in anthropology. Offered through UCL Anthropology and supported by the generous funding of the Sigrid Rausing Trust, these prestigious scholarships are designed to support outstanding scholars pursuing innovative and impactful research at one of the world’s leading anthropology departments.

Named in honor of the renowned anthropologist Mary Douglas, whose groundbreaking work transformed the understanding of culture, risk, symbolism, and social institutions, the scholarship aims to continue her legacy by supporting the next generation of anthropological researchers.

Applications are now open for students wishing to commence their doctoral studies in September 2026, with successful candidates joining UCL’s vibrant academic community and benefiting from world-class supervision, interdisciplinary collaboration, and extensive research resources.

About the Mary Douglas Research Scholarships

UCL Anthropology has announced two fully funded Mary Douglas Research Scholarships for doctoral candidates beginning their PhD journey in 2026. The scholarships are intended to support highly motivated researchers who demonstrate strong academic potential and propose original research projects capable of contributing significantly to anthropological knowledge.

As one of the most respected anthropology departments globally, UCL offers an intellectually stimulating environment where students engage with leading academics and researchers across a wide range of anthropological disciplines.

The scholarship recognizes academic excellence, originality of thought, and the potential of applicants to make meaningful contributions to the field of anthropology.

Scholarship Benefits

Successful candidates will receive comprehensive financial support for a period of three years.

The funding package includes:

  • Full coverage of Home (UK) tuition fees.
  • A maintenance stipend provided at UK Research and Innovation (UKRI) minimum rates.
  • Access to UCL’s extensive academic resources and research facilities.
  • Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration across departments and research centers.
  • Supervision from internationally recognized scholars in anthropology.
  • Membership in one of the world’s most prestigious research communities.

The scholarship significantly reduces financial barriers, allowing students to focus fully on their research and academic development.

Research Areas Supported

Applications are welcomed across the broad spectrum of anthropological research. UCL encourages proposals in areas including:

  • Social Anthropology
  • Cultural Anthropology
  • Medical Anthropology
  • Digital Anthropology
  • Visual Anthropology
  • Evolutionary Anthropology
  • Environmental Anthropology
  • Material Culture Studies
  • Interdisciplinary Anthropological Research

Applicants are encouraged to develop innovative projects that demonstrate originality, relevance, and methodological rigor.

Eligibility Requirements

To be considered for the Mary Douglas Research Scholarships 2026, applicants must:

  • Meet UCL’s academic requirements for admission to a PhD programme in Anthropology.
  • Demonstrate strong academic achievement.
  • Submit a compelling and original research proposal.
  • Show evidence of research potential and scholarly excellence.

The scholarship is primarily intended for applicants who qualify for Home (UK) tuition fee status.

However, overseas applicants are also welcome to apply. It is important to note that scholarship funding for tuition fees is capped at the Home fee level. Therefore, international students who are classified as overseas fee-paying students may need to secure additional funding to cover any difference between Home and Overseas tuition rates.

Fee status will be determined according to UCL’s standard procedures and relevant UK government regulations.

Application Documents Required

Applicants must submit a complete PhD application package, which includes:

1. Research Proposal

A maximum one-page research proposal outlining:

  • Research objectives
  • Originality of the project
  • Methodology
  • Feasibility of the study

Applicants should clearly indicate that they wish to be considered for the Mary Douglas Scholarship.

2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

A maximum three-page CV highlighting:

  • Academic achievements
  • Research experience
  • Conference presentations
  • Peer-reviewed publications
  • Relevant professional experience

3. Academic Transcripts

Applicants must provide undergraduate transcripts and postgraduate transcripts where applicable.

4. Supervisor Support Letter

A letter of support from a prospective supervisor within UCL’s Anthropology Department.

5. Academic References

Names and contact details of two academic referees who can comment on the applicant’s academic ability and research potential.

6. Personal Statement

A personal statement of up to 500 words describing:

  • Why the applicant meets the scholarship criteria.
  • Relevant academic and professional experiences.
  • Motivation for pursuing doctoral research.
  • Future academic and career aspirations.

Selection Process

Applications will be evaluated primarily on:

  • Academic merit.
  • Quality and originality of the proposed research project.
  • Feasibility of the research.
  • Research potential of the candidate.
  • Strength of supporting documents and references.

Competition for these scholarships is expected to be highly competitive, making it essential for applicants to submit well-developed and compelling applications.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit their complete application package to UCL Anthropology.

Applications and inquiries should be sent via email.

Applicants must include the subject line:

“Mary Douglas Scholarship”

Why Apply for the Mary Douglas Research Scholarships?

The Mary Douglas Research Scholarships offer far more than financial support. They provide an opportunity to study at one of the world’s leading universities, work alongside distinguished anthropologists, and contribute original research to pressing global conversations.

For students passionate about understanding human societies, cultures, health systems, environments, technologies, and social change, this scholarship offers an outstanding platform to launch a successful academic and research career.

By joining UCL Anthropology, scholars become part of a dynamic intellectual community dedicated to advancing anthropological knowledge and addressing contemporary global challenges through rigorous research and innovative thinking.

Important Deadline 

16 June 2026

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here

For more global opportunities, click here.

The post Mary Douglas Research Scholarships 2026 at UCL: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Anthropology in the United Kingdom appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Food Forum 2026 Call for Submissions: Global Opportunity for Youth-Led Bioeconomy Projects Driving Agrifood Systems Transformation
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
Young innovators, entrepreneurs, organizations, and change-makers working in the bioeconomy sector now have an exciting opportunity to gain international recognition and contribute to shaping future global policies. The World Food Forum (WFF) Youth Assembly, in collaboration with the Office of Climate Change, Biodiversity and Environment of the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations […]

The post World Food Forum 2026 Call for Submissions: Global Opportunity for Youth-Led Bioeconomy Projects Driving Agrifood Systems Transformation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young innovators, entrepreneurs, organizations, and change-makers working in the bioeconomy sector now have an exciting opportunity to gain international recognition and contribute to shaping future global policies. The World Food Forum (WFF) Youth Assembly, in collaboration with the Office of Climate Change, Biodiversity and Environment of the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), has officially launched a global call for submissions to identify and showcase youth-led bioeconomy projects that are transforming agrifood systems around the world.

This initiative seeks to amplify youth voices, highlight innovative solutions, and gather evidence-based insights that will contribute to future policy recommendations for governments, international organizations, and key stakeholders involved in agrifood systems transformation.

About the Opportunity

The World Food Forum is a global platform hosted by the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO). It works to accelerate progress toward sustainable development by engaging youth, innovators, researchers, policymakers, investors, and communities in creating solutions for global food and agricultural challenges.

Through this call for submissions, the WFF Youth Assembly aims to identify and document successful youth-led bioeconomy initiatives that are contributing to more sustainable, resilient, and inclusive agrifood systems.

The initiative recognizes that young people are playing a critical role in developing innovative approaches to agriculture, forestry, fisheries, aquaculture, biodiversity conservation, and related sectors. By gathering information about these projects, the organizers hope to strengthen youth participation in policy discussions and create recommendations that influence decision-making at national, regional, and international levels.

Objectives of the Call

The call for submissions has several important objectives:

1. Highlight Youth Contributions

The initiative seeks to showcase the contributions of young people, especially young women, who are advancing bioeconomy solutions that support sustainable agrifood systems transformation.

2. Promote Youth-Led Innovation

The organizers want to identify and celebrate projects that create employment opportunities, strengthen local economies, improve livelihoods, and generate positive community impacts.

3. Identify Challenges and Barriers

The survey will help uncover challenges faced by young people in accessing:

  • Finance and investment opportunities
  • Capacity development programs
  • Technical support and training
  • Resources and infrastructure
  • Decision-making platforms and policy spaces

Understanding these barriers will help inform future recommendations that support youth engagement in the bioeconomy sector.

What Is Bioeconomy?

Bioeconomy refers to the sustainable production, utilization, and conservation of biological resources to create products, services, and economic value while protecting the environment.

Examples include:

  • Sustainable agriculture practices
  • Circular economy initiatives
  • Renewable biological resources
  • Agricultural biotechnology
  • Biodiversity conservation projects
  • Sustainable fisheries and aquaculture
  • Bio-based products and innovations
  • Waste-to-resource solutions
  • Climate-smart agricultural technologies

Projects operating in these areas are encouraged to apply.

Why This Opportunity Matters

The information collected through this global initiative will not simply remain in a report. Instead, the findings will contribute directly to the development of youth-led policy recommendations that will be presented to decision-makers at multiple levels.

These recommendations will inform discussions within FAO Technical Committees on:

  • Agriculture
  • Forestry
  • Fisheries
  • Aquaculture

The findings may also contribute to discussions at future Global Bioeconomy Summits and other international policy platforms.

This means participants have an opportunity to influence conversations that shape the future of agrifood systems worldwide.

Who Can Apply?

The call is open to:

  • Young people aged 18–35
  • Youth-led organizations
  • Youth-led companies
  • Youth collectives
  • Youth initiatives
  • Youth movements
  • Formal and informal youth groups

Applicants from every region of the world are encouraged to participate.

Eligible Projects

Projects and initiatives must:

  • Demonstrate measurable impact
  • Showcase innovation
  • Have potential for scalability and replication
  • Focus on agrifood systems transformation
  • Be led by young people or youth-led entities

Relevant sectors include:

  • Agriculture
  • Forestry
  • Fisheries
  • Aquaculture
  • Biodiversity
  • One Health
  • Agrifood value chains
  • Climate-smart agriculture
  • Sustainable food systems

What Information Will Be Collected?

Participants will complete a global survey designed to gather both quantitative and qualitative information.

The survey will assess:

  • Project impact
  • Innovation and creativity
  • Scalability potential
  • Job creation outcomes
  • Income generation opportunities
  • Success factors
  • Challenges and barriers to implementation

The information collected will help build a stronger evidence base for youth engagement in bioeconomy development.

What Happens After Submission?

Following the survey process, participants will be invited to participate in an online consultation.

During this consultation, participants will:

  • Review preliminary findings
  • Validate emerging insights
  • Discuss priorities for action
  • Help shape youth-led policy recommendations

These recommendations will then be presented by youth representatives during international forums and policy discussions involving governments, international organizations, and other stakeholders.

Language Requirements

Submissions are accepted in all official United Nations languages:

  • Arabic
  • Chinese
  • English
  • French
  • Russian
  • Spanish

This ensures broad global participation and inclusivity.

Benefits of Participating

Participants stand to gain several advantages, including:

  • International visibility for their projects
  • Recognition within global agrifood and bioeconomy networks
  • Opportunity to contribute to global policy recommendations
  • Engagement with international experts and stakeholders
  • Participation in consultation processes
  • Networking opportunities with youth leaders worldwide
  • Contribution to future Global Bioeconomy Summit discussions
  • Increased exposure for innovative youth-led initiatives

Application Deadline

Interested applicants must submit their projects no later than 1 July 2026.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here.

The post World Food Forum 2026 Call for Submissions: Global Opportunity for Youth-Led Bioeconomy Projects Driving Agrifood Systems Transformation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Boston University Non-Resident Global Health Politics Fellowship 2026–2027: Fully Explained Guide for PhD Students, Postdocs, and Early-Career Scholars
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The Global Health Politics Workshop at Boston University is now accepting applications for the 2026–2027 Non-Resident Global Health Politics Fellowship. Sponsored by the Pardee Center for the Study of the Longer-Range Future, this prestigious fellowship provides an outstanding opportunity for doctoral students, postdoctoral researchers, and early-career academics to engage with leading scholars, receive mentorship, expand […]

The post Boston University Non-Resident Global Health Politics Fellowship 2026–2027: Fully Explained Guide for PhD Students, Postdocs, and Early-Career Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Global Health Politics Workshop at Boston University is now accepting applications for the 2026–2027 Non-Resident Global Health Politics Fellowship. Sponsored by the Pardee Center for the Study of the Longer-Range Future, this prestigious fellowship provides an outstanding opportunity for doctoral students, postdoctoral researchers, and early-career academics to engage with leading scholars, receive mentorship, expand their professional networks, and contribute to critical discussions shaping the future of global health policy.

Applications are open until July 3, 2026, at 8:00 PM EST, and selected fellows will participate in a one-year academic engagement program designed to strengthen scholarship and collaboration in the growing field of global health politics.

About the Global Health Politics Workshop

The Global Health Politics Workshop serves as an interdisciplinary academic platform dedicated to understanding how political, social, and institutional factors influence health outcomes worldwide. The workshop explores how research in global health politics can contribute to addressing some of the world’s most pressing health challenges.

The program focuses on major issues including:

  • Viral pandemics and global preparedness
  • Non-communicable diseases
  • Antimicrobial resistance
  • Universal health coverage
  • Global health governance
  • Power inequalities between the Global North and Global South
  • International health policy and decision-making

By bringing together scholars from diverse backgrounds, the workshop creates opportunities for meaningful dialogue, collaboration, and intellectual exchange on topics that affect populations across the globe.

What Makes This Fellowship Unique?

Unlike many academic fellowships that require relocation, the Global Health Politics Fellowship is entirely non-residential. Participants are not required to move to Boston or reside near the university.

Instead, the fellowship leverages virtual technology to connect fellows with faculty members, workshop participants, guest speakers, and members of the Steering Committee. This format allows scholars from around the world to participate while maintaining their existing academic or professional commitments.

The fellowship is particularly valuable for emerging scholars seeking to build academic visibility and strengthen their research within an international network of experts.

Fellowship Responsibilities

Selected fellows will become active members of the Global Health Politics Workshop community during the 2026–2027 academic year.

Fellows are expected to:

  • Attend all eight workshop talks held throughout the academic year.
  • Participate in four workshop sessions each semester.
  • Join either virtually through Zoom or attend in person if based in Boston.
  • Engage actively in workshop discussions and question-and-answer sessions.
  • Participate in optional one-on-one meetings with visiting speakers.
  • Receive mentorship and constructive feedback on ongoing research projects.
  • Collaborate and engage with other fellows and members of the Steering Committee.
  • Contribute to intellectual discussions surrounding contemporary global health issues.

These responsibilities are designed not only to deepen participants’ knowledge but also to create opportunities for professional growth and long-term scholarly collaboration.

Fellowship Benefits

The fellowship offers a variety of academic and professional benefits that can significantly enhance a scholar’s career trajectory.

Selected fellows will receive:

Access to a Prestigious Academic Network

Participants will join a vibrant community of researchers and practitioners working in global health politics. This network can facilitate future collaborations, publications, and career opportunities.

Mentorship from Leading Scholars

Fellows will receive guidance and feedback from experienced faculty members and experts in the field, helping them refine research projects and strengthen academic contributions.

Engagement with Distinguished Speakers

The workshop regularly hosts guest speakers and leading researchers whose expertise spans multiple dimensions of global health policy and politics.

Increased Professional Visibility

Participation in the fellowship can enhance fellows’ academic profiles by providing opportunities to present ideas, discuss research, and engage with influential scholars.

Research Development Support

Constructive feedback from mentors and peers can help fellows strengthen manuscripts, dissertation chapters, policy analyses, and other scholarly work.

Modest Financial Support

Fellows will receive a stipend of approximately $350.

Please note that participants located in the United States on certain visa categories may only receive the stipend if they possess legal authorization to do so.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must satisfy one of the following academic categories:

  • Current PhD students in good standing
  • Postdoctoral fellows conducting research related to global health politics
  • First-year professors

Applicants should also demonstrate a strong interest in the politics of global health, broadly defined.

The program welcomes interdisciplinary perspectives and encourages applications from scholars whose research engages with political, social, governance, policy, or institutional dimensions of health.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following documents:

1. Cover Letter (PDF)

The cover letter should include:

  • One paragraph explaining why you wish to be selected as a Global Health Politics Fellow.
  • One to two paragraphs describing your research interests.
  • An explanation of how participation in the fellowship aligns with your broader academic and career goals.

2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

Applicants must submit an up-to-date CV outlining their educational background, research experience, publications, presentations, and other relevant achievements.

Important Application Notice

Applicants should carefully prepare original application materials.

The fellowship announcement specifically notes that applications generated by Artificial Intelligence (AI) will not be considered during the selection process.

Applications should be submitted directly to: Joseph Harris Director, Global Health Politics Workshop.

Selection Timeline

The selection process will proceed according to the following schedule:

  • July 2026 to early August 2026: Application review period
  • Mid-to-late August 2026: Notification of selected fellows

Successful applicants will then join the workshop for the 2026–2027 academic year.

Why You Should Apply

For doctoral students, postdoctoral researchers, and early-career academics working in global health politics, this fellowship offers a rare opportunity to engage directly with leading experts while remaining based anywhere in the world. The combination of mentorship, scholarly networking, research feedback, and professional development makes this fellowship an excellent platform for advancing both academic careers and contributions to global health policy.

As global health challenges continue to grow in complexity, opportunities such as the Boston University Non-Resident Global Health Politics Fellowship play an important role in supporting the next generation of scholars dedicated to improving health outcomes and governance worldwide.

Application Deadline: July 3, 2026 (8:00 PM EST)

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here.

 

The post Boston University Non-Resident Global Health Politics Fellowship 2026–2027: Fully Explained Guide for PhD Students, Postdocs, and Early-Career Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Chapin Hall Careers 2026: Join a Leading Child and Family Policy Research Organization with Remote Work Opportunity.
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
Finding a meaningful career that combines research, public policy, social impact, and professional growth can be challenging. For professionals seeking to create lasting change in the lives of children, families, and communities, Chapin Hall offers a unique opportunity to contribute to evidence-based solutions that influence policy and practice across the United States. Chapin Hall is […]

The post Chapin Hall Careers 2026: Join a Leading Child and Family Policy Research Organization with Remote Work Opportunity. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Finding a meaningful career that combines research, public policy, social impact, and professional growth can be challenging. For professionals seeking to create lasting change in the lives of children, families, and communities, Chapin Hall offers a unique opportunity to contribute to evidence-based solutions that influence policy and practice across the United States.

Chapin Hall is an independent policy research center affiliated with the University of Chicago. Since its establishment, the organization has built a strong reputation for producing rigorous research, practical tools, and policy recommendations that improve child welfare systems, youth development initiatives, community support structures, and family well-being.

Through its fully remote work model, commitment to diversity and inclusion, and investment in employee development, Chapin Hall has become an attractive employer for professionals interested in research, policy analysis, data science, public health, social work, and related disciplines.

About Chapin Hall

Chapin Hall is dedicated to helping children, youth, families, and communities thrive. The organization works closely with policymakers, practitioners, government agencies, nonprofits, and philanthropic organizations to generate actionable research and practical solutions to some of society’s most pressing challenges.

The institution’s work spans several critical areas, including:

  • Child welfare systems
  • Youth homelessness
  • Family support services
  • Community capacity building
